summary: Phase 3 testing completed: Backend progress sync, skeleton loading states, and reduced-motion support all working correctly. All 13 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend testing verified all Phase 3 features including session ID display, sync indicator, progress persistence, skeleton loading, and confetti with reduced-motion support.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/progress saves progress with sessionId, completedSteps, step1Choice, currentStep
Backend: GET /api/progress/{session_id} returns saved progress
Backend: GET /api/progress/unknown_session returns 404 as expected
Backend: POST /api/progress updates existing progress (upsert working correctly)
Backend: Reset progress clears backend data correctly
Backend: All 8 steps endpoints return correct data
Backend: Director levels endpoint returns 9 levels
Frontend: Skeleton loading state appears during step transitions (data-testid='step-skeleton')
Frontend: Sidebar shows session ID in footer with font-mono styling (truncated with '...')
Frontend: Sidebar shows sync indicator when progress is being saved
Frontend: Progress syncs to backend when completing a step (verified via backend API)
Frontend: Reset Progress clears both localStorage and backend progress
Frontend: All 8 steps load correctly with full content
Frontend: Step navigation (Previous / Complete & Continue) works correctly
Frontend: Step 1 choice cards work and show selection state
Frontend: Copy Prompt button works (data-testid='prompt-copy-button')
Frontend: Step 8 Finale shows confetti animation (visible in screenshot)
Frontend: Step 8 Finale shows congratulations message and video embed
Frontend: Progress persists after page refresh
Frontend: Director level progression works (shows correct level based on completed steps)
Frontend: useReducedMotion hook properly detects prefers-reduced-motion media query
Frontend: Animations are conditionally disabled in StepContent.js when reduced motion is preferred
Frontend: Confetti is conditionally disabled in FinaleStep.js when reduced motion is preferred
Frontend: Framer-motion animations detected and working (7 elements with transform/opacity)
